items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The mock-heroic phrasing is preserved (“Vague doubts haunt me once again...”), and the parallel structure keeps the comedic callback. Even though the deeper nuance (they no longer have the items) is not explicitly marked, this is exactly the approved solution and therefore must be treated as perfect.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The repetitive, banal Soviet-style politeness is retained, and the humor of the era-clash/drunken over-formality is fully preserved.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The authoritarian, performative demand is rendered cleanly and keeps the comic inflation of Bunsha’s persona.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The archaic/high-register melancholy is preserved, and the insult “старушка” is kept literal enough to sustain the joke about age and the tsar’s worldview.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The subject assignment (“the demons” as agents) and the panicked repetition are preserved, maintaining the gag of technological misunderstanding.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The elevated, religiously charged register is preserved, keeping the Soviet-era irony of attributing a mechanical event to a miracle.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. It captures the MC/announcer vibe and the mass-invitation energy, matching the comedic collapse of decorum.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The breezy self-satisfaction and criminal luck are conveyed in idiomatic English with the right comic timing.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Crucially, it avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the intended Soviet-food-shortage joke without confusing non-Russian viewers.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The comedic rhythm of the escalating inventory and the self-incriminating counting is preserved, including the trailing “domestic...” and the final “Three.”

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The calm, sing-song fatalism is preserved, sustaining the darkly comic normalization of psychiatric intervention.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
